channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Who Was Joshua Baer, Capital Factory CEO Killed In Texas Plane Crash

media:content.media:description: In 2009, Baer founded Capital Factory in Austin, Texas.

item.description: Baer was traveling in a business jet with five other people when it crashed onto Loop 20, a major highway in Laredo and caught fire.
item.content:encoded: Baer was traveling in a business jet with five other people when it crashed onto Loop 20, a major highway in Laredo and caught fire.

item.title: Hormuz Reopening To Release Wave Of Oil Supply, Depress Prices

media:content.media:description: Reopening of Hormuz could unleash 93 million barrels of stranded non-Iranian barrels from the Gulf.

item.description: Middle Eastern crude oil markets could come under further pressure if the Strait of Hormuz reopens on Friday following
item.content:encoded: Middle Eastern crude oil markets could come under further pressure if the Strait of Hormuz reopens on Friday following

item.title: Milan Probes Tram Drivers' Chat Over CCTV Images Of Woman Passengers

media:content.media:description: Surveillance cameras are installed on public transport to protect both passengers and staff.

item.description: The case came to light in recent days after a woman travelling on a tram in Milan noticed an off-duty driver, still in uniform, sitting next to her and viewing a chat on his phone containing images focusing on parts of women's bodies filmed on public transport.
item.content:encoded: The case came to light in recent days after a woman travelling on a tram in Milan noticed an off-duty driver, still in uniform, sitting next to her and viewing a chat on his phone containing images focusing on parts of women's bodies filmed on public transport.

item.title: Opinion: Trump's Iran War: Was It Worth It?

media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump signs the Iran Memorandum of Understanding at Versailles in France

item.title: A History Of Iran's Nuclear Program And Tensions With US

media:content.media:description: US and Iran signed the interim deal remotely on Wednesday.

item.description: On February 28, 2026, Israel and the United States launched a war on Iran, killing Khamenei in the conflict's first moments.
item.content:encoded: On February 28, 2026, Israel and the United States launched a war on Iran, killing Khamenei in the conflict's first moments.

item.title: Indian-Origin Billionaire To Get Richer After Deal With Musk's SpaceX

media:content.media:description: Aman serves as Anysphere's Chief Operating Officer (COO).

item.description: With this buyout, each founder is expected to receive about $2.7 billion worth of SpaceX stock.
item.content:encoded: With this buyout, each founder is expected to receive about $2.7 billion worth of SpaceX stock.

item.title: Robots Pour Cocktails And Run Marathons, But Still Can't Multitask

media:content.media:description: A robot acts in the physical world, so its mistakes can hurt someone

item.description: Elon Musk loves to show off his Optimus prototype, recently filmed jogging in short strides.
item.content:encoded: Elon Musk loves to show off his Optimus prototype, recently filmed jogging in short strides.

item.title: Between War And Peace: Trump Invoked Weapons Production Law Before Iran Deal
item.description: By most estimates the US has fired billions worth of interceptors, missiles, and rockets, as well as jet fuel and other operational costs over the past four months of fighting in Iran and the Middle East region.
item.content:encoded: By most estimates the US has fired billions worth of interceptors, missiles, and rockets, as well as jet fuel and other operational costs over the past four months of fighting in Iran and the Middle East region.

item.title: Why India Has A Seat At The G7 Table Without Being A Member

media:content.media:description: India's economic rise has made it impossible for global powers to ignore it

item.description: India is not a formal member of the G7, which includes the US, UK, Canada, France, Germany, Italy and Japan. Yet, it is regularly invited as a special guest, showing how much importance the group now gives to New Delhi's voice.
item.content:encoded: India is not a formal member of the G7, which includes the US, UK, Canada, France, Germany, Italy and Japan. Yet, it is regularly invited as a special guest, showing how much importance the group now gives to New Delhi's voice.

item.title: Who Is Noam Shazeer? AI Pioneer Behind Gemini Now Joining OpenAI

media:content.media:description: Noam Shazeer worked on an AI chatbot called Meena at Google.

item.description: His exit comes less than two years after Google spent about $2.7 billion in a deal to bring him back to the company.
item.content:encoded: His exit comes less than two years after Google spent about $2.7 billion in a deal to bring him back to the company.

item.title: "What To Believe?" US Order On AI Models Leaves Anthropic Workers Confused
item.description: Nearly a week after the surprise order, many workers say they still have little clarity on why the restrictions were imposed.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a week after the surprise order, many workers say they still have little clarity on why the restrictions were imposed.

item.title: Indian Teen Dies While Trying To Save Mother Who Fell From Horse Carriage In US

media:content.media:description: The accident is the latest in a series of mishaps involving carriage horses.

item.description: The horse carriage accident in Central Park caused the death of Indian teenager Romanch Mahajan.
item.content:encoded: The horse carriage accident in Central Park caused the death of Indian teenager Romanch Mahajan.

item.title: Facial Recognition On Public Buses? US City Sparks Debate Privacy Debate

media:content.media:description: The transportation authority would archive the regular video footage on a local server for up to 5 years.

item.description: Images captured by cameras aboard the buses would immediately be checked against any active alerts, generated when a missing person, banned rider or someone on a law enforcement watch list designated by the transit authority is identified.
item.content:encoded: Images captured by cameras aboard the buses would immediately be checked against any active alerts, generated when a missing person, banned rider or someone on a law enforcement watch list designated by the transit authority is identified.

item.title: An Ancient Oak Tree Said To Have Sheltered Legendary Robin Hood Has Died

media:content.media:description: Tree experts found the root system of 'Major Oak' strangled and starved.

item.description: The 1,200-year-old Major Oak in Sherwood Forest is believed to have died after it didn't sprout leaves this spring, the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds said.
item.content:encoded: The 1,200-year-old Major Oak in Sherwood Forest is believed to have died after it didn't sprout leaves this spring, the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds said.

item.title: One Of The World's Most Important Climate Threats Has Image Problem

media:content.media:description: Predicted outcomes remain uncertain and some scientists are more hopeful than others

item.description: Many dynamic processes shape this ocean current, so we still don't know exactly how fast the circulation will change or even its future trajectory.
item.content:encoded: Many dynamic processes shape this ocean current, so we still don't know exactly how fast the circulation will change or even its future trajectory.

item.title: Why China's New Mega Dam On Brahmaputra Is Worrying India

media:content.media:description: Construction work of a dam in Yarlung Tsangpo River has picked up pace in recent months

item.description: China is building the world's largest hydroelectric dam in the lower parts of the Yarlung Tsangpo River in Tibet, about 50 kilometres from the border with India.
item.content:encoded: China is building the world's largest hydroelectric dam in the lower parts of the Yarlung Tsangpo River in Tibet, about 50 kilometres from the border with India.

item.title: Inside Chaotic US-Iran Talks That Let Trump Claim Victory, But Not Yet Peace

media:content.media:description: Justin Gaethje celebrates during the UFC fight on the South Lawn of the White House

item.description: The Iran war had already cost the US tens of billions of dollars, strained munitions stocks and its alliances, sent pump prices soaring and roiled the global energy market.
item.content:encoded: The Iran war had already cost the US tens of billions of dollars, strained munitions stocks and its alliances, sent pump prices soaring and roiled the global energy market.

item.title: UK Teen's "Stare At Wall" Comment On Social Media Ban Sparks Debate

media:content.media:description: UK teen's remark has gone viral on social media.

item.description: UK's social media ban is partly inspired by Australia, which became the first country to ban social media access for under-16s in December.
item.content:encoded: UK's social media ban is partly inspired by Australia, which became the first country to ban social media access for under-16s in December.

item.title: Iran Calls Deal "Failure" For US, Says Hormuz Won't Return To "Pre-War" Era

media:content.media:description: Ghalibaf clarified that the Strait of Hormuz will not return to the pre-war conditions.

item.description: Iran's top negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, however, said that Iran is engaging diplomatically with the US from a "position of strength".
item.content:encoded: Iran's top negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, however, said that Iran is engaging diplomatically with the US from a "position of strength".

item.title: "Very Wise Man Once Said...": US Reporter Plays Back Trump's 2020 Iran Tweet
item.description: Trump routinely criticises American media for spreading 'fake news' about his administration, referring to reportage critical of the president's handling of the economy and foreign policy.
item.content:encoded: Trump routinely criticises American media for spreading 'fake news' about his administration, referring to reportage critical of the president's handling of the economy and foreign policy.
